title: feat(ibg-repository): repository readiness report aggregation (IBG-1 PR G)
intent: Introduce a deterministic AGGREGATION layer that composes the three merged upstream artifacts — a RepositoryChangeProposal (PR A), its RepositoryExecutionPlan (PR E), and its RepositoryProposalEvaluation (PR F) — into a single content-addressed RepositoryReadinessReport a human reviewer can read as one coherent picture. It aggregates and presents; it manufactures no new finding, score, severity, recommendation, approval status, or narrative interpretation. It never edits an artifact, generates a commit, executes git, inspects a worktree, invokes a subprocess, creates a pull request, alters governance, promotes evidence, or authorizes engineering. It is a SEPARATE downstream consumer of the merged proposal + plan + evaluation contracts: it does not modify, wrap, or attach to the PR-D pipeline, the PR-E planner, or the PR-F evaluator, and nothing aggregates implicitly as a side effect. The report is content-addressed (rpt- id), byte-stable for identical artifacts, copies canonical upstream values verbatim, and terminates at the human-review boundary. This PR is STACKED on PR F (feature/ibg-repository-proposal-evaluation, PR #223): RepositoryProposalEvaluation is a required upstream contract, so this branch is cut from the rebased F head and cannot merge until F lands. Against F's head the diff is exactly this PR's seven files; against origin/main it additionally shows F's nine files until F merges.
behavior_change: Adds two additive modules (repository_readiness_report.py contract + readiness_report_builder.py builder) plus their two test modules under the existing services/api/app/ibg_repository/ and tests/ibg_repository/ trees, an architecture doc, and this per-PR manifest, and additively re-exports the PR-G API from __init__.py. No existing runtime path imports the report or builder; no existing behavior, schema, authority, or governance changes, and there is NO change to any upstream module (unlike PR F, this PR needs no extraction — it reuses GOVERNED_PROVENANCE_FIELDS from proposal_evaluator and READINESS_SUMMARIES from repository_proposal_evaluation exactly as exported). The builder is the report's only construction path: build_repository_readiness_report(proposal, execution_plan, evaluation) proves the artifacts describe ONE coherent chain before copying any value — the full lineage chain (proposal.proposal_id == execution_plan.proposal_id == evaluation.proposal_id AND execution_plan.execution_plan_id == evaluation.execution_plan_id) plus a provenance cross-check that the plan's provenance_reference agrees field-for-field with the proposal's target binding across the four evidence-owned fields. Any lineage or provenance mismatch is a construction error (ReadinessReportError), never a partial, warning-only, or 'incomplete' report; 'incomplete' describes evaluated readiness and does not legitimize mismatched artifacts. The report copies execution_plan.planning_summary into execution_summary (a documented rename), evaluation.evaluation_summary and evaluation.readiness_summary verbatim, projects governance findings into a frozen RepositoryGovernanceSummary(finding_count, check_ids) preserving the evaluation's upstream canonical order without re-sorting, and copies every canonical evaluation metric (completeness_score and the four counts) verbatim without recompute. readiness_summary is validated against F's closed complete/incomplete vocabulary and rejected otherwise. report_sections is a closed, builder-owned, frozen four-section vocabulary in fixed order (proposal, execution, evaluation, provenance); serialization is byte-identical for semantically identical inputs, and readiness_report_id = 'rpt-' + sha256(canonical_content)[:16] with created_at excluded from identity, hash, and canonical form. An AST-precise test forbids both G modules from importing subprocess/os/shutil/tempfile/network/GitHub clients or the PR-B git_runner and worktree machinery, and a further test asserts the report is not wired into the pipeline or evaluator.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Creates repository_readiness_report.py: the frozen, content-addressed RepositoryReadinessReport dataclass; the frozen RepositoryGovernanceSummary(finding_count, check_ids) structural projection; the frozen RepositoryReadinessSection(section_key, entries) record; the closed section-key vocabulary (proposal|execution|evaluation|provenance) with REPORT_SECTION_ORDER; READINESS_REPORT_CONSTITUTIONAL_CLASSIFICATION ('aggregation_readiness_report__no_approval_authority') and the READINESS_REPORT_ID_PREFIX 'rpt-'; ReadinessReportError; validate_readiness_summary() (rejects any value outside F's complete/incomplete); and to_canonical_dict()/to_audit_dict()/compute_report_hash() with readiness_report_id derived as 'rpt-' + sha256(canonical_content)[:16] (created_at excluded from identity and canonical form; sections serialized as an ordered [name, value] list so the builder's fixed order is preserved). Creates readiness_report_builder.py: build_repository_readiness_report(proposal, execution_plan, evaluation) and validate_repository_readiness_report(). _require_coherent_chain() fails closed on None/wrong-type inputs, on any lineage-ID disagreement across the three artifacts, and on any provenance disagreement between the plan's provenance_reference and the proposal's target binding (iterating GOVERNED_PROVENANCE_FIELDS reused from proposal_evaluator). The builder copies execution_plan.planning_summary -> execution_summary (documented rename), evaluation.evaluation_summary and evaluation.readiness_summary verbatim, projects governance check_ids in upstream order without re-sorting, and builds the provenance section solely from the plan's provenance_reference (the proposal binding is used only to validate it, never as a second source). __init__ additively re-exports the PR-G API. Two test modules (37 tests) bind: frozen contract/summary/section, content-addressed id stable across created_at and across independent content-addressed rebuilds, id changes on a semantic-field change, canonical dict excludes created_at while audit dict includes it, JSON round-trip, canonical section order, verbatim summary/metric/provenance/proposal copying, governance check_ids in exact upstream order with agreeing count, the full lineage chain rejected on each of the three possible ID mismatches, provenance disagreement rejected, None and wrong-type inputs failing closed on every slot, inputs unmutated, the validator's tamper/type/count/order/empty-identity branches, no decision vocabulary in the report's own fields, the AST-precise no-git/filesystem/GitHub/network import invariant on both modules, and the report not wired into the pipeline or evaluator. New-module coverage is 99% (repository_readiness_report.py 100%, readiness_report_builder.py 97% — the two uncovered lines are a defensive canonical-serialization guard unreachable while all canonical content is str/int). The architecture doc is added under docs/architecture, including the summary-field mapping, the vocabulary boundary, the lineage/provenance guarantees, and how a reviewer should read a report and what it explicitly does not decide.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No aggregation layer existed. PR A-F shipped the proposal, worktree, review-bundle, thin orchestration, descriptive execution-planning, and observational evaluation contracts; each terminates at the human-review boundary, and none of them presents the proposal + plan + evaluation as one coherent, verifiable artifact with a single content-addressed identity. This layer composes the already-merged contracts and copies only what those artifacts already declare: it invents no finding, score, severity, confidence, readiness classification, or judgment, duplicates no proposal/planning/evaluation logic, and does not modify or attach to the PR-D pipeline, the PR-E planner, or the PR-F evaluator. Its readiness_summary is a strict projection of F's value, rejected if outside F's vocabulary; its governance_summary is a structural projection of F's findings preserving F's ordering; its evaluation metrics are copied without recompute. It is not redundant with the fail-closed builders: those enforce their own preconditions at construction time, whereas this builder independently proves the three SUPPLIED artifacts form one coherent lineage-and-provenance chain before aggregating them — an independent cross-artifact check, not a restatement of any single builder's preconditions. It preserves provenance and the human-review boundary and adds a distinct downstream presentation capability rather than re-implementing an existing one.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant_stacked_note: Stacked on PR F (#223). RepositoryProposalEvaluation and GOVERNED_PROVENANCE_FIELDS are required F contracts, so G is cut from the rebased F head and cannot merge until F lands. After F merges, G rebases onto the new origin/main before final review; at that point the against-main diff reduces to exactly these seven files.

commands_run: python -m coverage run -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/test_repository_readiness_report.py tests/ibg_repository/test_readiness_report_builder.py --no-cov -q  &&  coverage report --include=*/ibg_repository/repository_readiness_report.py,*/ibg_repository/readiness_report_builder.py  ->  99% (contract 100%, builder 97%)
python -m pytest tests/ibg_repository/ --no-cov -q  ->  502 passed (PR A + B + C + D + E + F + G together on the rebased-F substrate, no regressions; F substrate baseline was 465)
python scripts/ci/check_cbsp21_patch_input.py --base 148f602c --head HEAD  (base = rebased PR-F head, G's effective base while stacked)
python scripts/ci/check_cbsp21_gate.py --changed-files <G-only diff vs F head>

files.intent: Frozen, content-addressed RepositoryReadinessReport contract + RepositoryGovernanceSummary + RepositoryReadinessSection, the closed section vocabulary, and the readiness-summary projection guard.
Deterministic aggregation builder: (proposal, execution_plan, evaluation) -> RepositoryReadinessReport, enforcing the full lineage chain and provenance cross-check before copying canonical values.
Additively re-export the PR-G readiness-report API.
Contract serialization, determinism, identity, and vocabulary tests.
Builder lineage/provenance enforcement, verbatim-copy, section-fidelity, validator, and import-boundary tests.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest for PR G.

findings.title: Aggregation could drift into decision-making or provenance discovery
findings.detail: A report that presents readiness is one vocabulary change away from approving, and an aggregator that carries provenance is one import away from discovering it. Mitigated structurally rather than by convention: the classification is aggregation_readiness_report__no_approval_authority; readiness_summary is a strict projection of F's complete/incomplete and is rejected if outside that vocabulary; governance_summary is a bare count + check_ids with no severity; evaluation metrics are copied without recompute; report_sections is a closed builder-owned vocabulary callers cannot extend; there is no free-form field; and AST-precise tests forbid both modules from importing subprocess/os/shutil/tempfile/network/GitHub clients or the PR-B git_runner and worktree machinery, so the report can only carry provenance the upstream artifacts supplied, never discover it.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Additive, aggregation-only. Copies every value from the already-merged proposal + plan + evaluation; invents no finding, score, severity, confidence, readiness classification, or judgment. Separate downstream consumer — does not modify or attach to the PR-D pipeline, the PR-E planner, or the PR-F evaluator, and is not wired into any of them. No git/filesystem/GitHub/network/authority; the No-Git boundary is enforced on THIS PR's modules, not claimed retroactively for the whole package (PR-B git utilities legitimately exist). Proves one coherent lineage-and-provenance chain before aggregating, failing closed on any mismatch rather than emitting a partial report. Terminates at the human-review boundary. Stacked on PR F (#223); rebases onto origin/main after F merges.
